texto: They say a picture is worth a thousand words, but a word is worth a million pictures. A billion. As many as there are people who know the word. Each has their own picture, their own meaning of it, in their heads. It's theirs. It's unique. And yet they share it with everyone else. And every time they use a word, a single word, they contribute to the creation of the soul of us all.
explicacao: Citação de Richard Tillotson sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “They say a picture is worth a thousand words, but a word is worth a million pictures. A billion. …” articula reflexao, language, pictures em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como pictures e words.

texto: Why were things funny? Was God laughing at us? Or was laughter the dispensation of God? Maybe laughter itself was Godlike. When we laugh, we rise above pain. We rise above indignity. We even rise above incredulity. We "get it." Maybe in the way God "gets it.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Richard Tillotson que provoca temas ligados a humor. Ao questionar “Why were things funny? Was God laughing at us? Or was laughter the dispensation of God? Maybe lau…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, god, laughter, com foco em laughter.

texto: University departments wanted to hire sober, rational researchers and teachers. You were allowed revelatory, apocalyptic experiences only after you had tenure.
